intro.uz: AJ Viktor Franklning “Man's Search for Meaning” kitobidan gapiradi: baxt va muvaffaqiyatni quvib boʻlmaydi — ular oʻzimizdan kattaroq maqsadga xizmat qilganda oʻzi keladi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasi mutafakkirlarning fikrini yetkazishga xizmat qiladi: oʻzlashtirma gap va zamon siljishi (Frankl said that…) hamda bayon feʼllari (argue / claim / suggest / point out). Chris haqidagi POV hikoyada ham buni eshitasiz.
intro.en: AJ talks about Viktor Frankl's “Man's Search for Meaning”: happiness and success cannot be chased — they come as a side effect of serving a cause bigger than yourself. This week's two grammar topics help you report that wisdom: reported speech with backshift (Frankl said that…) and reporting verbs (argue / claim / suggest / point out). You'll hear it in the POV story about Chris too.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida boshqa birovning aytganini toʻgʻri zamon siljishi bilan qayta ayta olasiz (“He said that he was…”) va fikrni argue / claim / suggest / point out feʼllari bilan aniq qolipda bayon qila olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can report what someone said with correct backshift (“He said that he was…”) and present an idea accurately using argue / claim / suggest / point out with the right pattern.

grammar.titleUz: Oʻzlashtirma gap: bayon va zamon siljishi (backshift)
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 3 — report other people's views and studies accurately
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1→B2: I can report a statement, shifting the tense and time words correctly
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida bayon qilganda zamon oʻzgarmaydi: “U baxtni quvib boʻlmaydi dedi” — feʼl hozirgi zamonda qoladi va “dedi” oxirda keladi. Ingliz tili esa “that”ni boshga qoʻyadi va ichkaridagi feʼlni bir qadam orqaga suradi: cannot → could not, is → was, will → would. Shuningdek now→then, here→there, this→that.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ Frankl said me that happiness is a byproduct. ✅ Frankl said that happiness was a byproduct. / ✅ Frankl told me that happiness was a byproduct. “say”dan keyin odam kelmaydi (tell + odam); va bosh feʼl oʻtmishda boʻlsa, ergash gap feʼli ham orqaga suriladi (is → was).

examples.en: Frankl said that happiness could not be pursued.
He told his students that success would follow them.
examples.uz: U oʻquvchilariga muvaffaqiyat ularga ergashadi, dedi.

examples.en: She said that she felt free then.
examples.uz: U oʻsha payt oʻzini erkin his qilganini aytdi.

exercises.prompt: Frankl said that happiness ___ be pursued.
exercises.hintUz: bosh feʼl said (oʻtmish) → cannot orqaga suriladi: could not

exercises.prompt: He ___ me that meaning was the most important thing.
exercises.hintUz: odam (me) kelsa → tell; “said” dan keyin odam kelmaydi

exercises.prompt: She said that she ___ tired that day.

exercises.promptUz: Bugun kimdir aytgan bitta gapni ovoz chiqarib qayta ayting: “She/He said that … (was/would/could) …”.
exercises.hintUz: that + zamonni bir qadam orqaga suring; say (odamsiz) yoki tell + odam

grammar.titleUz: Bayon feʼllari: suggest / argue / claim / point out + qoliplar
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 3 — discuss ideas in academic register (argue/claim/suggest)
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can choose the right reporting verb and its pattern (that / -ing / obj+to)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada deyarli hamma bayon “dedi” yoki “maslahat berdi” bilan tugaydi. Ingliz tili feʼlni boshga qoʻyadi va har biriga alohida qolip beradi: argue/claim/point out + that; suggest + -ing yoki that; advise/urge/admonish + obj + to. POV hikoyada bu present perfectda yangraydi: “His mother has admonished him to eat vegetables.”
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ She suggested me to try a new strategy. ✅ She suggested trying a new strategy. / ✅ She suggested that I try. “suggest” obj+to qolipini olmaydi — undan keyin -ing yoki that keladi (bu oʻzbekcha “menga … qilishni taklif qildi”dan soʻzma-soʻz oʻgirilganda chiqadigan xato).

examples.en: Frankl argued that we create meaning from our experiences.
examples.uz: Frankl maʼnoni tajribamizdan oʻzimiz yaratamiz, deb daʼvo qildi.

examples.en: His mother suggested trying a new strategy.
examples.uz: Onasi yangi yoʻlni sinab koʻrishni taklif qildi.

examples.en: His mother admonished him to eat vegetables.
examples.uz: Onasi uni sabzavot yeyishga qattiq undadi.

exercises.prompt: Frankl ___ that success is a byproduct of helping others.
His mother suggested ___ a new strategy.
His mother admonished him ___ his vegetables.

exercises.promptUz: Bir fikrni uch xil feʼl bilan ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “He said that… He argued that… He pointed out that…”.
exercises.hintUz: argue/claim/point out + that; suggest + -ing; advise/urge + obj + to

vocab.defEn: to strongly urge or tell someone to do something
vocab.example: Frankl admonished his students not to aim at success.

vocab.defEn: to chase something and try hard to get it

vocab.uz: keyin yuz bermoq, natijada kelib chiqmoq
vocab.defEn: to happen afterwards as a result
vocab.example: Success will ensue if you focus on a bigger goal.

vocab.defEn: an extra result you did not plan for
vocab.example: Happiness is an unintended side effect of helping others.

vocab.defEn: a result you did not want or expect
vocab.example: Eating only steak had an unintended consequence.

vocab.defEn: an extra thing produced besides the main one
vocab.example: Success is a byproduct of serving a bigger cause.

vocab.defEn: to act according to your sense of right and wrong
vocab.example: You must follow your conscience, even when it is hard.

vocab.defEn: a goal or purpose bigger than your own interests
vocab.example: Dedicate yourself to a cause greater than yourself.

vocab.defEn: two opposite things that are both true at once
vocab.example: It's a paradox: helping others helps you the most.

vocab.defEn: to discover a reason or purpose that matters to you
vocab.example: Try to find meaning in learning English.

englishpod.title: The Office — Interview Skills: Education Background

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda Rebekka ish intervyusida taʼlimi va koʻngilli faoliyatlari haqida gapiradi — u uysizlarga yordam berishni “really fulfilling” deb ataydi. Franklning gʻoyasiga mos: oʻzidan kattaroq ishga xizmat qilish maʼno beradi. Sizningcha, koʻngilli boʻlish (volunteering) nega odamga qoniqish keltiradi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue Rebecca talks about her education and volunteering in a job interview — she calls helping the homeless “really fulfilling.” It fits Frankl's idea: serving something bigger gives life meaning. Why do you think volunteering feels fulfilling?

keyVocab.defEn: the main subject you study at university
a second, smaller subject you study at university
a mark or award given for excellent academic work
a person who loves reading and studying
to bring a good result after effort
very interested in or eager to do something
to do something willingly without being forced or paid
making you feel happy and satisfied

quiz.q: Where is the most isolated inhabited place on the planet?
quiz.qUz: Sayyoradagi eng chekka, odam yashaydigan joy qayerda?
options: Tristan da Cunha in the South Atlantic
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: c) Tristan da Cunha (Janubiy Atlantikadagi orol). Sam a) Antarktida dedi va xato qildi. Orolda 300 dan kam odam yashaydi va u yerga Janubiy Afrikadan kema bilan 6 kun suzib borish kerak.

vocab.defEn: far away from other places and people
a fixed, oversimplified idea about a type of person or group

vocab.uz: biror narsadan azob chekmoq, tinimsiz bezovta boʻlmoq
vocab.defEn: to be troubled by something again and again
to understand or find the answer to something
to control something so it stays balanced

funEnglish.title: Viktor Frankl: Finding meaning in life's hardest moments

speakingPrompt.en: Think of a writer, teacher or thinker who influenced you. Report what they said or argued, and explain the meaning you found in it. What did they claim, and what did they suggest you do?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: oʻzlashtirma gap va zamon siljishi (He said that he was…; She told me that…) hamda bayon feʼllari (argue / claim / suggest / point out — masalan, “He argued that…”, “She suggested trying…”).
